storylines.why_now: Explosives detonated near the Four Seasons hotel where the French president was staying during his Syria state visit.
A major structural shift in Gaza's post-war governance, announced this week.

storylines.moment: Trump declares the Iran ceasefire "over," strikes resume
storylines.why_now: Announced from the NATO summit in Ankara; the U.S. launched fresh strikes after Iran targeted commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

storylines.moment: Pressure mounts on Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner
storylines.why_now: Facing calls to drop his 2026 bid as new allegations emerge.
Survivors are still searching for missing family members as the toll continues to rise.

storylines.moment: Manhattan high-rise evacuated over collapse fears
storylines.why_now: A 37-story building under construction was evacuated after columns buckled and floors sagged between the 21st and 26th floors.

storylines.moment: Dow drops, oil surges as Iran ceasefire collapses
storylines.why_now: The Dow fell over 1% and Brent crude jumped 5.7% after renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities rattled markets.
Bezos's space company closed its first outside fundraising round at that valuation.

storylines.moment: Manhattan office leasing hits a 20-year high
storylines.why_now: A sharp counter-signal to years of "office apocalypse" narratives about downtowns emptying out for good.
Ends government-requested limits that had constrained the model's availability.

storylines.moment: $30 million orbital debris salvage mission launches
storylines.why_now: A robotic "lifesaver" is heading up this week to retrieve stranded hardware in orbit.
JAXA's probe revealed a near-Earth asteroid shaped unlike anything scientists expected.
A familiar galaxy rendered "far richer" than in any prior image of it.
China's probe arrives after a journey of more than 1 billion kilometers.
Steps down shortly after the historic crewed lunar flyby earlier this year.

storylines.moment: Solar flares raise aurora odds across the U.S.
storylines.why_now: Three flares this week widen the typical latitude where the northern lights are visible.
Down 2-0, Messi and Enzo Fernández pulled off a stoppage-time World Cup win to reach the quarterfinals.
Brazil–Norway, Mexico–England, and Colombia–Switzerland decided the last quarterfinal spots.

storylines.moment: Rare early Declaration of Independence copy found
storylines.why_now: A volunteer at Britain's National Archives discovered it during America's 250th-anniversary year.

storylines.moment: Theory spreads that the Swift-Kelce wedding rollout is itself the story
storylines.why_now: A conspiracy theory that the multimillion-dollar rollout is its own PR campaign is gaining real traction.

storylines.moment: Blake Lively excluded from the Swift-Kelce wedding
storylines.why_now: Reported as the final straw in a friendship once called Hollywood's most bulletproof.

storylines.moment: Prince Harry loses UK press-intrusion lawsuit
storylines.why_now: A British judge ruled against Harry and others in the case against the Daily Mail's publisher.
A contestant's apology tour has become its own referendum on parasocial reality-TV culture.
A hosting incident sparked a multiplatform debate about award-show culture.

storylines.moment: Empire State Building antenna climbers get engaged, then arrested
storylines.why_now: Two rooftoppers scaled the antenna and unfurled a pro-peace banner before the proposal — and the arrest.
